objections.description: The plan defers critical architectural decisions to the backend audit but then proceeds to design two parallel implementation paths. This is not 'grounded in the current codebase' — it's speculative. The entire document viewer architecture (TOC generation, section anchoring, collapse strategy) depends on whether the backend returns structured JSON or flat HTML. Designing both paths before the audit completes wastes effort and creates false confidence.
objections.suggested_fix: Move Section 5a implementation design AFTER Section 0 audit is complete. Replace the conditional with a single implementation approach that will be validated during the audit. If audit reveals flat HTML, add a dedicated parsing service with proper AST generation, not client-side regex.

objections.description: Chunked append strategy is underspecified for production. No specification for: (1) how IntersectionObserver interacts with browser Ctrl+F when not-yet-rendered content exists, (2) memory management when user scrolls through a 50,000-word document and DOM nodes accumulate, (3) how chunked rendering affects TOC scroll-sync and search highlight positioning. The claim 'no react-window because Ctrl+F' ignores that chunked append has the same problem — Ctrl+F won't find text in chunks not yet rendered.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify a hybrid approach: render all text content in a compressed form (hidden spans with textContent) for Ctrl+F compatibility, but only hydrate visible sections as rich DOM. Alternatively, pre-render entire document to an iframe with virtual scrolling. Address memory management explicitly — specify a maximum live DOM node budget and eviction strategy for scrolled-past content.

objections.description: localStorage persistence has no error handling, migration strategy, or corruption recovery. Reading position schema includes 'lastSectionId' but no document version hash — if a DOU document is republished with structural changes, stale section IDs will break navigation. The 100-item LRU cap doesn't account for localStorage quota errors or JSON serialization failures.
objections.suggested_fix: Add schema version field. Add document hash (publication date + ID) to reading positions for staleness detection. Wrap all localStorage operations in try/catch with quota-exceeded fallback. Add a schema migration utility that runs on app init. Specify what happens when localStorage is full (graceful degradation to session-only state).

objections.description: 18-24 working days is unrealistic for the scope. Document viewer alone (8 days) includes: progressive rendering system, TOC generation from parsed structure, search highlighting with navigation, cross-reference parsing and linking, URL deep linking, typography controls, reading progress persistence. Each of these is a non-trivial feature requiring testing. No time allocated for: code review, QA testing, accessibility testing with actual screen readers, performance regression testing, bug fixes from integration.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit time for testing and iteration: add 2 days after each major feature (viewer, search) for integration testing and bug fixes. Add 2 days total for accessibility testing with VoiceOver/TalkBack. Total realistic timeline: 26-32 days solo. Alternatively, cut scope by deferring cross-reference linking and reading progress persistence to a second phase.

objections.description: Regex parsing for legal references is notoriously unreliable. Brazilian legal citations include edge cases like 'Art. 5º-A', '§ 3º-B', 'Lei nº 8.666/93' vs 'Lei 8.666/1993' vs 'Lei nº 8.666, de 21 de junho de 1993'. No consideration for false positives (e.g., 'Art. 5' appearing in quoted text, not as a reference). Real-time lookup for each parsed reference could be slow.
objections.suggested_fix: Use a proper parser with grammar-based extraction (nearley.js or similar). Build a reference index at document ingestion time (backend) rather than parsing on every view. Cache resolved links per-document with TTL. For MVP, only link references within the same document; inter-document links are Tier 2.

objections.description: Client-side term matching fallback is underspecified. For a 50,000-word document, matching a query string against all text nodes is O(n) with significant memory pressure. No handling for: (1) stemming (licitação vs licitações), (2) case sensitivity, (3) phrase matching, (4) boolean operators in query. The 'N matches found' indicator could show misleading counts if client-side matching differs from backend highlight logic.
objections.suggested_fix: If backend doesn't return highlights, DO NOT attempt client-side matching for complex queries. Show a toast: 'Highlights unavailable for this query.' Only attempt client-side matching for single-term queries with simple ASCII characters. Document the limitation explicitly.

objections.description: Section 3 states 'body reading: 1rem (16px floor enforced)' and also 'Font size: 16 / 18 / 20px (3 stops)'. If 16px is the floor, the user control starting at 16px makes sense, but the specification doesn't clarify whether the user selection overrides the floor or if the floor applies to the minimum control value. Additionally, 'Mobile density: achieved through collapsible sections and tighter vertical padding — never by reducing font size below 14px for any interactive element' contradicts the 16px floor.
objections.suggested_fix: Clarify the type scale hierarchy: base reading size (user-controllable: 16/18/20px), minimum interactive element size (14px absolute minimum, but prefer 16px), and mobile density approach (padding reduction, not type reduction). Specify that user font size selection only applies to document body text, not UI chrome.

objections.description: 'Show 5 most recent publications from each section' and '234 new publications today' require API endpoints that may not exist. The backend audit doesn't include checking for 'recent publications' or 'daily counts' endpoints. The plan assumes these exist without verification.
objections.suggested_fix: Add 'recent publications endpoint' and 'publication counts by date' to the Section 0 backend audit checklist. If these don't exist, the empty state should degrade gracefully: show only the search input with a static prompt, no dynamic data. This is a hidden dependency that could block the entire empty state design.

objections.description: Snap points at 40% viewport height on a 375px mobile screen = 150px, which may not be enough for a usable TOC. The handle zone at 48px might be difficult for users with motor impairments. No specification for virtual keyboard handling — when a sheet contains an input field and the keyboard appears, the sheet should resize or scroll.
objections.suggested_fix: Change TOC sheet minimum snap to 50% on viewports under 400px. Increase handle zone to 56px. Add explicit behavior for sheets containing inputs: when keyboard appears, sheet should auto-snap to maximum height and scroll to the focused input. Specify `visualViewport` API usage for keyboard detection.

objections.description: 'Remove separate Home route' but the document viewer is a separate route. The navigation model is unclear: user is on Search (home), taps a result, goes to /document/:id — where is the back button? How does the bottom tab bar behave on the document route? If 'Search' tab is tapped while on document, does it save position and return home?
objections.suggested_fix: Specify navigation flow explicitly: Document viewer has its own header with back arrow that returns to search with scroll position preserved. Bottom tab bar remains visible on document route but Search tab acts as a 'home' button (prompts to save reading position, then navigates). Add a navigation diagram.

suggestions.description: The plan touches many components that share state (search query, active filters, reading positions, saved documents). No specification for state management approach: React Context, Zustand, Jotai, or URL as source of truth. This architectural decision should be explicit before implementation begins.
